title: ONNX Inference Metadata
description: Portable, runtime-agnostic inference metadata for ONNX generative models. All top-level sections are optional, and unknown fields are permitted for forward-compatible schema evolution.

hardware_requirements.description: Minimum and beneficial hardware capabilities used for distribution matching.

kv_cache.description: KV-cache storage, quantization tolerance, and operational semantics.

model.description: Build-time model properties and runtime-configurable capabilities.

pipeline.description: Declarative multi-model pipeline and its dataflow graph.

quantization.description: Model weight quantization intent, independent of the packed representation.

required_capabilities.description: Capability identifiers that a runtime MUST support or refuse to load the model.

speculative.description:
Standalone speculative proposer declaration.

This is the preferred native source for speculator discovery;
HuggingFace `config.json` is a compatibility fallback. The deprecated
`speculator_config` alias is accepted on input.

allOf.description:
Standalone speculative proposer declaration.

This is the preferred native source for speculator discovery;
HuggingFace `config.json` is a compatibility fallback. The deprecated
`speculator_config` alias is accepted on input.

strategy.description: Generic inference strategy, including speculative decoding.

structured_output.description: Structured-output formats and model training conventions.

fallback_behavior.description: Compatible attention behavior for runtimes that do not recognize `type`.

num_kv_heads.description: Number of key/value heads; required by runtimes that need explicit GQA dimensions.

sink_tokens.description:
Number of leading "attention sink" tokens always retained alongside the
sliding window (StreamingLLM). Only meaningful when `sliding_window` is
set; `null` or `0` disables sink retention. These first tokens stabilize
the attention distribution and are never evicted by the window.

sliding_window.description: Sliding-window length in tokens, or null for full-context attention.

type.description:
Attention architecture.

Canonical values include `multi_head`, `grouped_query`, and
`multi_latent`; future values are allowed when paired with a usable
`fallback_behavior`.

AttentionType.description: Attention architecture vocabulary with an extension branch.

chunk_size.description: Preferred number of prompt tokens processed in each prefill chunk.

DType.description: Scalar dtype vocabulary with common ONNX and runtime aliases.

DataflowEdge.description: Directed connection between two pipeline component ports.

device_transfer.description: Whether the runtime must move the value between execution devices.

dtype.description: Scalar or logical data type at the component boundary.

session.description: Named runtime session or pipeline component used as the producer.

HardwareRequirements.description: Model-side hardware requirements and distribution-matching hints.

beneficial_dtypes.description: Dtypes that improve performance or memory use but are not mandatory.

kv_cache_memory_per_1k_tokens_mb.description: Estimated KV-cache memory in MiB per 1,000 cached tokens.

min_memory_gb.description: Minimum aggregate accelerator or system memory in GiB.

min_tp_degree.description: Minimum useful tensor-parallel degree when tensor parallelism is selected.

required_dtypes.description: Dtypes the selected device or execution provider must support.

supports_tensor_parallel.description: Whether the model can be partitioned with tensor parallelism.

KvCacheOperations.description: Operational guarantees for mutable KV-cache state.

checkpoint_serializable.description: Whether checkpoints can be serialized for suspend/resume or migration.

fork_precision_policy.description: Precision policy for a copy-on-write fork, such as `inherit` or `highest`.

rewind_safe.description: Whether truncating cache state to an earlier token position is correctness-preserving.

KvCacheSpec.description: KV-cache storage, precision tolerance, and operational guarantees.

native_dtype.description: Native KV scalar dtype produced by the model before optional compression.

operations.description: Cache mutation and persistence operations known to be safe for this model.

quantization_tolerance.description: Independent precision tolerance for key and value tensors.

sensitive_layers.description: Layer indices that should retain high precision; negative indices count from the end.

KvComponentTolerance.description: Quantization tolerance for one KV-cache component.

default.description: Default minimum acceptable scalar dtype for this component.

quantization_axis.description: Quantization scaling axis, such as `per_tensor`, `per_channel`, or `per_token`.

KvQuantTolerance.description: Precision tolerance for key and value cache components.

LayerPrecisionOverride.description: Minimum precision required by a set of model layers.

layers.description: Non-empty layer-index list; negative indices count from the final layer.

min_precision.description: Minimum acceptable scalar dtype for the listed layers.

ModelCapabilities.description: Model properties that are baked into the graph or advertised as configurable.

max_sequence_length.description: Maximum total sequence length, in tokens.

runtime_configurable.description: Features that a serving runtime may configure at load time.

speculative.description: Built-in draft-head or self-speculative model properties.

MtpHiddenLayout.description: Layout of the target state consumed by an MTP sidecar.

oneOf.description: `[batch, sequence, hc_mult, hidden]` Mobius Hyper-Connection layout.

MtpKvMode.description: Lifetime declared for an MTP sidecar's private KV state.

oneOf.description: Reset sidecar KV at every target verification iteration.
Retain only KV corresponding to the accepted draft prefix.

MtpTargetInitializer.description: Exact target-model initializer reference used by an MTP sidecar.

name.description: Exact initializer name in the target ONNX graph.

source.description:
Initializer ownership source. The Phase-1 contract requires
`target_initializer`.

MtpWeightSource.description: Ownership source for an MTP shared weight.

oneOf.description: Borrow the named initializer from the target model package.

expected_acceptance_rate.description: Expected fraction of proposed tokens accepted, from 0.0 through 1.0.

optimal_k.description: Recommended number of draft tokens per verification step.

PhaseConfig.description: Phase gate for one pipeline component.

run_on.description: Pipeline phase in which the component runs.

PhaseRunOn.description:
Pipeline phase gate.

Known values are enumerated while future strings remain valid.

PipelineComponentSpec.description: One executable ONNX model in a pipeline.

device_preference.description: Optional execution or device preference declared by the model package.

filename.description: Non-empty ONNX filename relative to the model package root.

tokenizer.description:
Tokenizer filename relative to the package root.

If absent, loaders may use a shared top-level `tokenizer.json`.

PipelineSpec.description: Multi-model pipeline represented as a directed acyclic dataflow graph.

dataflow.description: Directed tensor or data edges between component ports.

models.description: Named model components in the pipeline DAG; at least one component is required.

phases.description: Optional per-component phase gating, keyed by component name.

strategy.description: Loop and execution strategy for the pipeline.

vision.description:
Vision-language model token-expansion contract.

When present, the engine uses these fields to replace each image
placeholder token in the prompt with `tokens_per_tile * num_tiles`
copies of that token before KV-cache allocation.

PipelineStrategy.description: Parameterized execution strategy for a pipeline or composite stage.

cfg_conditioning_input.description:
Denoiser conditioning input port zeroed for the unconditional pass of
classifier-free guidance. Required when `guidance_scale` != 1.0.

denoiser.description: Iterative or diffusion denoiser component name.

guidance_scale.description: Classifier-free guidance scale or equivalent strategy-specific multiplier.

inner.description:
Inner autoregressive decoder for a `nested_autoregressive` stage.

The code_predictor: for each outer frame it runs a short inner AR loop of
`num_code_groups` steps over the residual codebooks, seeded at inner step
0 by the outer decoder's `last_hidden_state` (routed via a dataflow edge
`outer.last_hidden_state -> inner.inputs_embeds`) and threading its own
per-step code embedding on later steps.

kind.description: Strategy family; determines which strategy-specific fields are meaningful.

max_tokens.description: Maximum number of tokens generated by an autoregressive stage.

num_code_groups.description:
Inner-loop depth (RVQ residual codebook count) for a
`nested_autoregressive` stage: the number of code tokens collected per
outer frame. Must be at least 1.

num_steps.description: Number of iterative or diffusion steps.

outer.description:
Outer autoregressive decoder for a `nested_autoregressive` stage.

The multi-decoder TTS (Qwen3-TTS-style) shape: one outer step is one
audio frame. The outer decoder (talker) produces a per-frame
`last_hidden_state` that seeds the inner loop (see `inner`).

pre_embedder.description:
Optional pre-embedder component driving the outer decoder (talker) of a
`nested_autoregressive` stage through `inputs_embeds` instead of
`input_ids`.

A real Qwen3-TTS talker is not driven by token ids: each step's
`inputs_embeds` is materialized from the PREVIOUS frame's codes as
`codec_sum(+ text_embed)` (where
`codec_sum = codec_embed(code_0) + Σ_i cp_codec_weights[i][codes[i+1]]`).
When this field names such a component (inputs
`frame_codes [batch, num_code_groups]` int64 `[+ text_embed [batch, 1,
hidden]]` → output `inputs_embeds [batch, 1, hidden]`), the runtime builds
the outer decoder's per-step `inputs_embeds` through it, keeping the engine
generic. Requires a dataflow edge
`{pre_embedder}.inputs_embeds -> {outer}.inputs_embeds`.

When absent the outer loop is `input_ids`-driven (backward compatible).

scheduler.description: Scheduler identifier for iterative or diffusion execution.

scheduler_config.description:
Optional diffusion scheduler applied to the denoiser's loop-carried
output (treating it as a noise prediction) each step.

stages.description: Ordered child stages for a composite strategy.

start_step.description:
First step index for a partial (img2img) denoise loop.

When set, the iterative loop runs `start_step..num_steps` instead of the
full `0..num_steps`, and the seed (`denoiser` sample input) is expected to
already be the encoded image noised to `timesteps[start_step]`. Matches
diffusers' img2img `get_timesteps(num_steps, strength)` skip. Default 0.

timestep_input.description:
Denoiser input port that receives the per-step timestep/sigma scalar.

When set, the iterative loop feeds this input a rank-1 `float32` value
each step (from `timesteps` when provided, otherwise the 0-based step
index), so a step-aware denoiser can condition on the current step.

timesteps.description:
Explicit per-step timestep/sigma schedule for an iterative strategy.

When present its length must equal `num_steps`; when absent the loop
uses the 0-based step index. Requires `timestep_input` to have any effect.

PipelineStrategyKind.description:
Pipeline execution strategy family.

Known values are enumerated while future strings remain valid.

PipelineStrategyStage.description: Named child stage of a composite pipeline strategy.

name.description: Non-empty stage name unique within its containing composite.

run_on.description: Optional phase gate for the stage.

PipelineVisionConfig.description:
Image placeholder token-expansion contract for encoder-free VLM pipelines.

Both fields must be set together; declaring only one is allowed for
forward compatibility but will cause an engine error at generation time
when `num_image_tiles` is supplied.

image_placeholder_token_id.description:
Token ID of the image placeholder in the tokenized prompt.

The engine replaces every occurrence of this token with the expanded
image token sequence before sequence-length and KV-cache sizing.

tokens_per_tile.description:
Number of image tokens each tile expands to.

The total expansion per placeholder is `tokens_per_tile * num_tiles`.

ProposalType.description:
Speculator proposal architecture.

Known spellings are enumerated in the generated schema while unknown
strings remain valid to preserve forward compatibility.

default.description: Default precision or quantization recipe for model weights.

QuantizationOverride.description: Precision override for selected layers or a named graph component.

component.description: Logical component path, for example `attention.qk` or `lm_head`.

layers.description: Layer indices to which the override applies; negative indices count from the end.

RuntimeConfigurable.description: Features whose concrete settings may be selected by the runtime.

chunked_prefill.description: Chunked-prefill support and preferred chunk size.

continuous_batching.description: Whether continuous batching may be enabled.

prefix_cache.description: Whether prefix caching may be enabled.

dtype.description: Non-empty list of supported KV-cache scalar dtypes, in preference order.

SchedulerSpec.description:
Diffusion scheduler configuration for an iterative strategy.

The runtime treats the denoiser's loop-carried output as a noise prediction
(or, for `masked_diffusion`, as token logits) and applies one scheduler step
per iteration. Supported `kind`s: `ddim`, `euler`, `dpmpp_2m` (image
diffusion, with optional Karras/exponential sigmas) and `masked_diffusion`
(discrete language diffusion).

block_length.description:
Semi-autoregressive block length for a `masked_diffusion` scheduler, in
tokens. When set (and smaller than the masked generation region), each
step only commits tokens inside the current left-to-right block, matching
LLaDA's semi-autoregressive remasking. Defaults to a single block
spanning the whole masked region.

mask_token_id.description:
Mask token id for a `masked_diffusion` (language-diffusion) scheduler:
each step commits the highest-confidence still-masked positions.

num_train_timesteps.description: Training timesteps the noise schedule was defined over (default 1000).

remasking.description:
Unmasking strategy for a `masked_diffusion` scheduler:
  * `"low_confidence"` (default) — LLaDA: each step commits the
    highest-confidence still-masked positions (confidence-ranked). Best
    for LLaDA checkpoints, but greedy/confidence-ranked decoding of other
    masked-diffusion LMs (e.g. MDLM) collapses into repetitive text.
  * `"random"` — MDLM-style ancestral: each still-masked position unmasks
    independently with the schedule probability `1/(steps_remaining)`,
    sampling its token from the model's categorical distribution (use
    `temperature: 1.0` for a true categorical sample). This per-position
    stochastic unmasking avoids the degenerate loops that confidence
    ranking produces. The mask token is never emitted.

temperature.description:
Sampling temperature for a `masked_diffusion` scheduler. `0` (default)
selects each masked position's argmax token deterministically; a positive
value applies Gumbel noise (`logits.exp() / (-log u)^temperature`) before
the argmax, matching LLaDA's `add_gumbel_noise`. Confidence used for
remasking is always the clean-softmax probability of the chosen token.

use_exponential_sigmas.description:
Use the exponential sigma spacing (`exp(linspace(log σ_max, log σ_min))`)
instead of linspace. Applies to `euler`/`dpmpp_2m`. Mutually exclusive
with `use_karras_sigmas` (Karras takes precedence).

use_karras_sigmas.description:
Use the Karras (arXiv:2206.00364, rho=7) sigma spacing instead of the
default linspace spacing. Applies to sigma-space schedulers (`euler`,
`dpmpp_2m`); the most popular ComfyUI scheduler for those samplers.

SharedKvGroup.description:
One shared-KV binding group for a shared-KV proposer.

A `shared_kv` proposer graph exposes `shared_kv.<name>.key` and
`shared_kv.<name>.value` inputs bound to slices of the target model's paged
KV cache. `target_layers` lists the target KV layer indices feeding this
slice.

target_layers.description: Target KV layer indices whose cache feeds this shared-KV slice.

SpeculativeModelInfo.description: Build-time support for self-contained speculative decoding.

has_draft_heads.description: Whether the exported graph contains Medusa/EAGLE/MTP-style draft heads.

self_speculative_depth.description: Early-exit layer depth usable for self-speculation.

SpeculatorConfig.description: Configuration published with a standalone speculative proposer model.

backbone_hidden_size.description:
Target backbone hidden size `H` shared with the proposer.

For `shared_kv`, `inputs_embeds` is `[B, q, 2*H]` and
`projected_state` is `[B, q, H]`.

embedding.description: Target embedding initializer shared with the MTP sidecar.

input_embedding.description:
Relative path (from the model directory) to the target model's raw
input-token embedding table, as a little-endian f32 matrix in
`[vocab_size, backbone_hidden_size]` order.

The `shared_kv` proposer builds each step's `inputs_embeds` as
`concat(target_input_embedding(last_token), hidden)`, so it must be able
to look up the target's input embedding of the last drafted/accepted
token. Required for the `shared_kv` proposer.

kv_mode.description:
Lifetime of the sidecar's KV state.

Defaults to `proposal_local`.

lm_head.description: Target LM-head initializer shared with the MTP sidecar.

logits_output.description:
Name of the proposer's draft-distribution output.

Defaults to `logits` for `shared_kv`.

allOf.description:
Proposal architecture used by the speculator.

The deprecated `method` alias is accepted on input.

model.description:
Relative path (from the model directory) to the proposer ONNX model.

Used by the `shared_kv` proposer to locate the
proposer graph. Optional for forward compatibility with proposer
families that do not ship a standalone model file.

mtp_hidden_output.description:
Sidecar output projected through the shared target LM head.

Defaults to `mtp_hidden`.

mtp_state_output.description:
Sidecar recurrent Hyper-Connection state output.

Defaults to `mtp_state`.

num_speculative_tokens.description:
Maximum number of tokens proposed per verifier step; defaults to 4.

The deprecated `tokens_per_step` alias is accepted on input.

projected_state_output.description:
Name of the proposer output threaded forward between steps.

Defaults to `projected_state` for `shared_kv`.

proposal_type.description:
Proposal architecture used by the speculator.

The deprecated `method` alias is accepted on input.

shared_kv.description:
Shared-KV binding groups consumed by the proposer.

Each group names an assistant input prefix
(`shared_kv.<name>.{key,value}`) and the target KV layer indices whose
cache feeds that slice. Empty for proposers that own their KV cache.

target_hidden_layout.description:
Layout of `target_hidden_output`.

Mobius MTP sidecars use `BSHC`: batch, sequence, Hyper-Connection lane,
hidden.

target_hidden_output.description:
Target decoder output carrying the recurrent MTP seed.

Defaults to `hidden_states` for `mtp`.

allOf.description:
Maximum number of tokens proposed per verifier step; defaults to 4.

The deprecated `tokens_per_step` alias is accepted on input.

verifier.description: Identity of the verifier model against which this proposer was trained.

vocab_size.description: Vocabulary size of the proposer's own `logits` output.

SpeculatorVerifier.description: Verifier identity embedded in a speculator package.

architectures.description: Verifier architecture class names, in preference order.

name_or_path.description: HuggingFace-style verifier repository name or local model path.

draft.description: Draft-token producer configuration for speculative decoding.

tokens_per_step.description: Number of draft tokens attempted per verification step.

StructuredOutputSpec.description: Structured-output capabilities and model formatting conventions.

stop_sequences.description: Literal token sequences that terminate a structured response.

supported_formats.description: Supported constraint formats, such as JSON Schema, regular expressions, or CFGs.

training_format.description: Format in which the model was trained to emit tool calls or structured values.

TensorDType.description: Tensor-boundary dtype vocabulary, including non-numeric pipeline values.

session.description: Named verifier session or pipeline component.
